features.definition: Primary product nature of the company.
features.decisionRule: Use marketplace if the UI is primarily about browsing a catalog of third-party offerings; physical if centered on a manufactured product; digital otherwise.

features.definition: High-level product vertical for grouping in tools and analysis (README Collection buckets plus automotive OEMs).
features.decisionRule: Use automotiveAndMobility for car manufacturers and mobility OEM marketing sites in this dataset. Otherwise assign one README Collection bucket (AI & Machine Learning; Developer Tools & Platforms; Infrastructure & Cloud; Design & Productivity; Fintech & Crypto; Enterprise & Consumer). Stripe is Infrastructure & Cloud in the README. New slugs: add to `scripts/extract_feature_table.py` README_COLLECTION_BUCKET_BY_SLUG; use unknown only as a temporary placeholder.

features.definition: Dominant UX mode implied by layout + component patterns.
features.decisionRule: browsingHeavy for feeds/grids of items; taskFocused for workflows/actions; creationTool for editors/canvases; docsHeavy for documentation-style density.

features.definition: How central imagery is to the interface and layout hierarchy.
features.decisionRule: imageFirst when key UI sections are structured around photography/illustration; textFirst when content is mostly typography/components; mixed otherwise.

features.definition: How the system uses color at the semantic/brand level.
features.decisionRule: singleAccent for mostly neutrals plus one primary; multiAccent for several distinct roles/brands; gradientLed when gradients are core; monochrome for near-black/white only.
technical when monospace or code-like styling is prominent; premium when display typography + spacing are luxury-coded; expressive when brand voice is strongly stylized; neutral otherwise.
dense for dashboards/docs with tight spacing; sparse for showroom-like layouts; balanced for typical SaaS marketing/UI.
flat when shadows are avoided and borders/opacity do the work; subtle for minimal shadows; layered for multi-depth stacks and heavy elevation language.

features.definition: Whether the design system is dark-first, light-first, or explicitly dual-mode.
features.decisionRule: darkFirst if the doc describes dark surfaces as the primary identity; lightFirst if it defaults to light surfaces; dual if both are first-class.

features.definition: Dominant geometry for components (corners, pills, chips).
features.decisionRule: pill if 999px/pill shapes dominate; rounded if consistent radii without full pills; sharp if corners are mostly tight/rectilinear; mixed if multiple strong geometries.

features.definition: How depth is expressed through shadow/ring/border techniques.
features.decisionRule: none if shadows are avoided; subtle if minimal single shadows; stacked if multi-layer shadow stacks are a signature; ring if inner/outer rings replace conventional shadows.

features.definition: Primary visual/content payload emphasized in the system's examples and patterns.
features.decisionRule: codeFirst for code blocks/terminal aesthetics; productScreenshots for UI-as-hero; photography for editorial/real imagery; illustration for illustrative systems; mixed otherwise.

features.definition: How much motion/animation is part of the system.
features.decisionRule: Use what the doc explicitly describes; unknown if not mentioned.

features.definition: Hypothesized dominant intent of the design system.
features.decisionRule: Infer only if multiple signals point the same way; otherwise unknown.

notesField.definition: Single free-text field for nuance/caveats (more robust than a one-liner; keep readable).
